search_results.title: Script to validate XML sitemaps against the XML schema
search_results.snippet: This guide describes how to validate XML sitemap files and XML sitemap index files against their XML schema using xmllint.

search_results.title: Protocol - sitemaps.org
search_results.snippet: The Sitemap protocol format consists of XML tags. All data values in a Sitemap must be entity-escaped. The file itself must be UTF-8 encoded.

search_results.title: jirkapinkas/sitemap_validator: Sitemap Validator - GitHub
search_results.snippet: This tool will crawl any sitemap.xml, validate, parse and check each URL, if a sitemap index is found it will be added for processing.

search_results.title: Free XML Sitemap Validator · Check Errors & Validate URLs Online
search_results.snippet: A sitemap validator is a tool that checks your XML sitemap for errors, validates URL format, and ensures compliance with the sitemaps.org protocol. It helps ...

search_results.title: XML Sitemap Validator | Free SEO Tools - BrightKeyword
search_results.snippet: Validate your XML sitemap against the sitemaps.org protocol. Check for errors, analyze URL distribution, and get a health score.

search_results.title: FAQ - sitemaps.org

search_results.title: Free XML Sitemap Validator | CommonNinja
search_results.snippet: This tool runs eight validation checks against the official Sitemap Protocol specification and Google Webmaster Guidelines to catch the most impactful issues.

search_results.title: sitemaps.org - Home
search_results.snippet: The Sitemaps protocol enables webmasters to information earch engine about pages on their site that are available for crawling.

message.content:
Below is an itemized list of **testable validation checks** derived directly from the official **sitemaps.org** protocol plus Google’s documentation, matching your requested items. For each: the check, what it verifies, and the exact authoritative URL.

---

### 1. Root `<urlset>` element is required and correctly formed with namespace

- **Check**  
  XML sitemap must:
  - Begin with an opening `<urlset>` tag  
  - End with a closing `</urlset>` tag  
  - Include the correct Sitemaps namespace as an `xmlns` attribute on `<urlset>` (typically `xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"`).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures the file is recognized as a valid sitemap document per the Sitemaps protocol and is structurally correct XML with the required root element and namespace declaration.[2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - sitemaps.org Protocol: `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - XML schema (indirectly confirms namespace): `http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9/sitemap.xsd` (linked from FAQ)[6]  

---

### 2. Required `<url>` container for each URL entry

- **Check**  
  Each URL in the sitemap must be contained in a `<url>` element that is a direct child of `<urlset>`.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that each listed URL is wrapped in the correct parent element so that search engines can parse individual entries.[2]  

- **Official URL**  
  - `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 3. Required `<loc>` element for each `<url>`

- **Check**  
  Every `<url>` element must contain exactly one `<loc>` child element.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures that each URL entry includes the required location tag; `<loc>` is the only mandatory child element according to the protocol.[2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 4. `<loc>` must contain a fully-qualified absolute URL including protocol

- **Check**  
  The content of `<loc>` must be a fully-qualified absolute URL that:
  - Includes the protocol (e.g., `http` or `https`)  
  - Is not a relative path (no `/page` type URLs).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures that sitemap URLs are valid absolute URLs as required by the protocol and FAQ, allowing search engines to fetch them correctly.[6][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (protocol and trailing slash requirement, protocol required): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol general description of URLs in `<loc>`: `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 5. `<loc>` URL must be URL-escaped / URL-encoded

- **Check**  
  The value in `<loc>` must be properly URL-escaped (URL-encoded) according to RFC‑3986 / RFC‑3987 and readable by the web server; e.g., spaces, non-ASCII and reserved characters encoded appropriately.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures all URLs are correctly encoded so that web servers and search engines can interpret them without errors; this is explicitly required for “all URLs (including the URL of your Sitemap).”[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (URL-escaped requirement): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - FAQ (references RFC‑3986 and RFC‑3987 for URLs/IRIs): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

*(Note: sitemaps.org requires URLs be “URL-escaped,” but it does not specify a maximum character length like 2,048; that limit is from common browser/implementation guidance, not from sitemaps.org or Google.)*

---

### 6. Optional `<lastmod>` element with W3C Datetime format

- **Check**  
  If `<lastmod>` is present, its value must conform to W3C Datetime / ISO 8601, e.g.:
  - `YYYY-MM-DD`  
  - `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD` such as `2004-09-22T14:12:14+00:00`.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that modification dates are in the correct standardized format so search engines can parse them; sitemaps.org explicitly instructs to use W3C Datetime encoding and notes allowable omission of the time component.[6][2]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (W3C Datetime encoding, example formats): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol (description of `<lastmod>`): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 7. Optional `<changefreq>` element with allowed values

- **Check**  
  If `<changefreq>` is present, its value must be one of the allowed tokens:
  - `always`  
  - `hourly`  
  - `daily`  
  - `weekly`  
  - `monthly`  
  - `yearly`  
  - `never`.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures `<changefreq>` uses only valid enumerated values defined in the protocol so that parsers can interpret or safely ignore them without errors.[2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (enumerated values for `changefreq`): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 8. Optional `<priority>` element with numeric range 0.0–1.0

- **Check**  
  If `<priority>` is present, its value must be a decimal number between `0.0` and `1.0` inclusive (e.g., `0.0`, `0.5`, `1.0`).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that `<priority>` values fall within the permitted numeric range defined by the sitemap protocol schema, avoiding invalid values that can cause parsing errors.[2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (description and range for `priority`): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 9. Maximum 50,000 URLs per sitemap file

- **Check**  
  Count the number of `<url>` entries in a single sitemap; it must not exceed **50,000**.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures the sitemap stays within the officially supported URL count limit; larger sets must be split across multiple sitemaps and optionally referenced from a sitemap index.[6][2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (50,000 URLs maximum per sitemap): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol (size and URL-count limits): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 10. Maximum 50 MB uncompressed file size per sitemap

- **Check**  
  The uncompressed size of the XML sitemap file must not exceed **50 MB (52,428,800 bytes)**.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that the sitemap respects the official file-size limit so search engines can reliably download and process it; larger collections need to be split.[6][2][5]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (50MB / 52,428,800 bytes limit): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol (size limits): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 11. UTF‑8 encoding requirement

- **Check**  
  The sitemap file must be encoded in **UTF‑8** (XML declaration `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>` should reflect this, and the actual byte encoding must match).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures the sitemap adheres to the required character encoding so that all data values, especially non‑ASCII characters, are parsed correctly by search engines.[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (“file itself must be UTF-8 encoded”): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - FAQ (“Your Sitemap files must use UTF-8 encoding”): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### 12. Entity-escaping of special characters in all data values (including URLs)

- **Check**  
  In all element contents (including `<loc>` URLs, `<lastmod>`, etc.), the characters:
  - `&`  
  - `'`  
  - `"`  
  - `<`  
  - `>`  
  must be properly entity-escaped as `&`, `'`, `"`, `<`, `>`.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures that the XML is well-formed and that special characters do not break parsing; sitemaps.org requires **all data values** in a sitemap to be entity‑escaped, and the FAQ lists the exact characters.[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (“All data values in a Sitemap must be entity-escaped”): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - FAQ (lists the required escapes and references XML standard): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### 13. All URLs must use the same host as the sitemap location

- **Check**  
  For a sitemap located at e.g. `http://www.example.com/sitemap.xml`, every `<loc>` URL must reside on the same host `www.example.com` (no `example.org`, `sub.example.com`, etc., unless the sitemap itself is on that host).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures compliance with the rule that all URLs in a sitemap must reside on the **same host** as the sitemap; this is explicitly stated in the sitemaps.org FAQ.[6][2]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (“All URLs listed in the Sitemap must reside on the same host as the Sitemap”): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol (note that URLs in sitemap must use same protocol and host as the sitemap): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 14. All URLs must use the same protocol as the sitemap location

- **Check**  
  If the sitemap is accessed via `http://` then all `<loc>` entries must use `http://`; if via `https://`, all `<loc>` entries must use `https://`.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that URLs in the sitemap use the same protocol as the sitemap file itself, in line with the sitemaps.org statement that all URLs listed “must use the same protocol (http, in this example) and reside on the same host as the Sitemap.”[2]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (same protocol requirement): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 15. Sitemap index file format: root `<sitemapindex>` and `<sitemap>` children

- **Check**  
  For sitemap index files:
  - Root element must be `<sitemapindex>` with the Sitemaps namespace (e.g., `xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"`).  
  - It must contain one or more `<sitemap>` child elements.  
  - Each `<sitemap>` must contain a `<loc>` element (and may contain an optional `<lastmod>`).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures sitemap index documents match the Sitemaps Index Protocol specification, allowing search engines to discover multiple sitemap files.[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol section “Sitemap index files”: `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - XML schema for sitemap index: `http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9/siteindex.xsd` (linked from FAQ)[6]  

---

### 16. Sitemap index: maximum 50,000 sitemap references and 50 MB uncompressed

- **Check**  
  In a sitemap index:
  - Number of `<sitemap>` entries must not exceed 50,000.  
  - Uncompressed file size must not exceed 50 MB (52,428,800 bytes).  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms the sitemap index adheres to the same size and entry-count constraints as a normal sitemap, as documented by sitemaps.org.[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (limits for sitemap index files): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - FAQ (size and URL limits, also applicable to indexes via protocol): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### 17. Sitemap index `<loc>` URLs must be URL-escaped and same host/protocol

- **Check**  
  For each `<sitemap><loc>` in a sitemap index:
  - URL must be fully-qualified, URL-escaped, and encoded per RFC‑3986/3987.  
  - URL must use the same protocol and host as the sitemap index file.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures that the sitemap index itself follows the same URL encoding and host/protocol rules as regular sitemaps, since the protocol and FAQ specify these requirements for “all URLs (including the URL of your Sitemap).”[2][6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - Protocol (URL-escaped requirement and same protocol/host rule): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  
  - FAQ (host requirement and RFC references): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### 18. XML must be well-formed and valid against the official schemas

- **Check**  
  - XML must be well‑formed (proper nesting, closing tags, encoding declaration, etc.).  
  - Optionally, validate against:
    - `http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9/sitemap.xsd` for sitemap files.  
    - `http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9/siteindex.xsd` for sitemap index files.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Ensures that the XML complies with the formal Sitemaps XML schemas, catching structural or datatype violations beyond simple well-formedness.[6][3]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (schema locations): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### 19. Compression format (if used) must be gzip

- **Check**  
  If the sitemap or sitemap index is served as a compressed file, the compression format must be **gzip** (`.gz`) as per the sitemaps.org recommendation.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Confirms that compressed sitemaps use a format search engines explicitly support and recommend in the FAQ.[6][2]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (“Please use gzip to compress your Sitemaps”): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  
  - Protocol (mentions compression support): `https://www.sitemaps.org/protocol.html`[2]  

---

### 20. Recommended location (root directory) – advisory, not hard validation

- **Check**  
  (Advisory / best-practice, typically not a hard “invalid/valid” check.) If present, verify that the primary sitemap is at the root (e.g., `http://example.com/sitemap.xml`), or otherwise note that non-root placement might restrict which host paths it can cover.  

- **What it verifies**  
  Helps enforce the recommendation that sitemaps be placed at the root to simplify coverage and permissions, as strongly recommended by the FAQ (though not mandated).[6]  

- **Official URL**  
  - FAQ (strong recommendation for root location): `https://www.sitemaps.org/faq.html`[6]  

---

### About the 2,048-character URL limit

Sitemaps.org and Google’s official sitemap documentation **do not specify** a 2,048-character maximum for `<loc>` values. The protocol only requires that URLs:

- Follow RFC‑3986/RFC‑3987  
- Be URL-escaped and properly encoded[2][6]  

If you implement a 2,048-character limit, it should be treated as a **tool-specific or infrastructure constraint**, not a protocol requirement, and you should label it accordingly in your validator rather than citing sitemaps.org or Google for it.
